violations.description: Demonstration of knowledge by person in charge. — --Person in charge did not demonstrate adequate knowledge of the food service code requirements. The prep cooler in the kitchen has been written up for not working in the 3 of the last four inspections. This cooler must be maintained in working order. It is the responsibility of the certified manager to ensure its continued operation.

Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. Certified manager did not ensure that employees knew how to ensure the prep cooler in the kitchen was working properly.

Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Witnessed employee cutting lemons for customer water with bare hands. Gloves must be worn when cutting these lemons.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. A bag a raw onions was stored in the walk in cooler touching a box of raw chicken. These must be separated to prevent contamination.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. The prep cooler in the kitchen had products temping between 49 and 52 degrees on the top and 50 degrees on the bottom part of the cooler. The air temp in the bottom of the cooler was 51 degrees. Two of the doors are bent and do not properly seal. The reach in cooler across from the flat grill had products temping between 47 and 49 degrees. The air temp in the cooler was 59 degrees. This cooler is also not working.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Food contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in good repair or sound condition. The green cutting board in the bar area for cutting limes has deep gouges with black build up. This is no longer a cleanable surface.--Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. The front doors of the prep cooler in the kitchen are bent and do not properly seal. These must be in good condition to ensure the proper operation of the prep cooler.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. Foil pans on the shelf above the tables in the stockroom were not inverted.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. The hand sink in the service area by the register had a pitcher of water stored in the sink.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. The hand sink in the service area with the soda dispenser did not have soap.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc.) were not maintained in good repair. Broken floor tile in front of the ice bin in the bar area.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Employee in kitchen did not wash hands after handling raw steaks and shrimp. He then went on the plate Cooked rice, beans, and cheese.

Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed using same pair of single-use gloves for more than one task. Witnessed Employee in kitchen use same pair of gloves to grab raw product then scoop cooked rice and beans to plate

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. A box of napkins was stored on the floor in the upstairs storage area.
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Available, knowledgeable, certified manager (4 pts deducted)
Clean clothes, hair restraints, fingernail maint., jewelry, eating/drinking (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Clean, proper use, storage, sanitizing solution (2 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
